description:
Parent spike: CMUX-16 (task_01KPHHQZA4XZTQD4BQCGQYC7FR). Full plan: .lattice/plans/task_01KPHHQZA4XZTQD4BQCGQYC7FR.md.

**Public charter (v3):** This ticket elevates **panes** to first-class, stable, mobile identities in c11mux. Panes hold their own IDs, persist across sessions, and move between frames and windows without runtime-object churn. Multi-frame / multi-window (Emacs-frames) is the first consumer of that primitive; v1.1 unlocks (`pane.attach`, `frame.mirror`, broadcast, `workspace.present`) follow from the same foundation. The product story is the primitive elevation; "multi-window" is the outward-facing demo.

**v1 envelope (this ticket):** 7–9 weeks across 6 phases. One c11mux instance that spans displays as one cohesive workspace with per-window independent sidebars. Runtime hotplug/hibernation and parked/headless pane states are explicitly NOT in v1 — tracked on CMUX-26.

## Primitive hierarchy (locked, use these terms)

**Window** (NSWindow) → **Sidebar** (window-local, selects workspace) → **Workspace** (process-scoped, owns pane IDs) → **WorkspaceFrame** (`frameId: UUID` identity, `{workspaceId, windowId}` as fields; one bonsplit tree per frame) → **Panes** (process-scoped in `PaneRegistry` — durable identity, pane-local metadata, pane-level scrollback) → **Surfaces** (process-scoped; PTY, Ghostty, MTL, WKWebView live here — the Surface is the runtime owner). **Tabs** are the UI affordance when a pane has >1 surface.

**Runtime ownership correction from earlier revisions:** PTY / Ghostty / MTL live on the **Surface**, not on the Pane. Pane groups 1..N surfaces and owns durable aggregates (scrollback). Resolution G2.2.

**`PaneAttachment` ledger** (introduced at Phase 2): process-scoped fact table `{ paneId, frameId, leafPath, orderingHint }` — single source of truth for "which pane is where." All cross-frame operations (`pane.move`, drag-drop, `workspace.spread`, placement events, CMUX-26 hotplug) read/write through the ledger. Resolution G2.4.

**Pane single-home at v1.** Each pane is attached to exactly one frame at a time. `workspace.spread all_on_each` is cut from v1 (Resolution G2.9); moves to v1.1 when multi-viewer is properly designed.

## Feature flag `CMUX_MULTI_FRAME_V1`

- **UX-gated only.** The Phase 2 registry refactor ships unconditionally; the flag gates only the visible multi-frame UX (Phase 3+).
- **Exposed in Settings.** Default OFF at v1 release, ON for nightly; opt-in adoption is the validation surface (no formal dogfooding cohort — Resolution G2.12).
- **Retirement criterion.** Removed in the first release tagged **≥30 days after Phase 6 lands on `main`** AND with no open P0 regressions filed against multi-frame in that window (Resolution G1.12).

## Phases

Execute in order. Phase boundaries are natural PR boundaries — each lands on main, green on CI, before the next starts. Phase 1 blocks Phase 2; Phase 2 blocks Phases 3–6 (3/4/5/6 parallelizable after Phase 2 lands).

### Phase 1 — Display registry + CLI surface (~1 week)

Foundation. No cross-window behavior; new commands unlock manual display assignment only (Resolution G1.7).

- `DisplayRegistry` enumerating `NSScreen`s with stable `display:<cmuxDisplayID>` refs, positional aliases, numeric indices.
- **Positional alias rules (Resolution G1.16):** `N=1` → only `left`; `N=2` → `left`/`right`; `N=3` → `left`/`center`/`right`; `N≥4` → positional aliases return `err(code: "ambiguous_position")`, only `display:<index>` or `display:<cmuxDisplayID>` valid.
- Socket: `display.list`, `display_ref` on `identify` / `tree`, `tree --by-display` flag. `cmux window new --display <ref>` unlocked here.
- **Perf canary baseline captured (Resolution G1.19):** keystroke→onscreen latency; `cmux tree` at 3 windows × 30 panes; `cmux identify` latency; frame creation latency; sidebar coalescing under status/log/progress burst. Re-run at the end of each subsequent phase; >20% regression triggers an investigation issue (not a phase block).

### Phase 2 — Pane-first registry refactor (~3 weeks, day-10 tripwire) (Resolution G1.5, G1.8)

Honest estimate: **3 weeks**, with a day-10 tripwire — if sub-PRs 1+2 aren't landed at review, the phase splits.

**Sub-PR ordering** (each lands green on CI with the flag off and on):

1. `WorkspaceRegistry` + `PaneRegistry` shells (facade pattern, no ownership moves). **Bonsplit pre-check (Resolution G1.4):** confirm no internal pane-ID uniqueness caches break under N controllers per workspace; if found, patch bonsplit. No user-visible change.
2. Move panel/surface lifecycle behind the facade. `Workspace.panels` / `Workspace.bonsplitController` route through the registries. UI unchanged.
3. `WorkspaceFrame` wrapping `BonsplitController`. `FrameID` becomes a UUID with `{workspaceId, windowId}` as fields (Resolution G2.3). `PaneAttachment` ledger lands here (Resolution G2.4). Still one frame per workspace at this sub-PR.
4. Rename `TabManager` → `WindowScope`. Session schema v2 migration + migrator framework (see plan §Session schema v2). Socket rename `workspace.move_to_window` → `workspace.move_frame_to_window` with deprecation shim.

**Phase 2 acceptance test matrix (Resolution G1.20):**
- Snapshot migration v1→v2 with duplicate-workspace fixture.
- Flag-off launch: full single-window regression suite + session restore + cross-tab drag-drop.
- Model invariant tests: `WorkspaceRegistry` / `PaneRegistry` / `PaneAttachment` consistency under add/remove/move; no stale frame references.
- Socket rename tests: both names resolve; deprecation notice appears on the old name.
- Concurrent `pane.move` same-pane idempotency (stub here; real cross-window test at Phase 3).

**Shim retirement (Resolution G1.17):** the `workspace.move_to_window` alias is removed in the release tagged **≥30 days after Phase 2 merges**. Pre-removal, run a documented consumer grep (Resolution G2.15) against Stage 11 siblings, cmux skill, `claude-in-cmux`, `cmuxterm-hq` CI; paste results into the shim-removal PR.

### Phase 3 — Cross-window pane migration (~1 week)

- Drag-drop + `pane.move` socket + `Cmd+Shift+Ctrl+<Arrow>` keybinding.
- **Drag-drop acceptance matrix** (10 scenarios, plan §3): two visible windows, cross-display, minimized target, cross-Space, fullscreen target, source/target window closes mid-drag, mixed-DPI, WKWebView portal, Mission Control interrupt. Failing scenarios downgrade to CLI fallback with user-visible message; CLI `pane.move` is always the supported path.
- **Keybinding audit (Resolution G1.15):** `Cmd+Shift+Ctrl+<Arrow>` vetted against Ghostty default keymap, common TUI shortcuts (cc, codex, vim, emacs-in-terminal, helix), macOS system bindings. Results in the phase's ticket scope.

### Phase 4 — Per-window independent sidebar (~1 week)

- `SidebarMode` enum with single case `.independent` at v1 (Resolution G1.13). No `.primary` / `.viewport` stubs; no `broadcastSelection(...)` no-op. Sync mode designed and built when it actually ships.
- Session persistence round-trips per-window selected workspace + `sidebarMode`; unknown values on decode coerce to `.independent` with a `dlog` warning.
- **Empty-window state (Resolution G2.7):** when a window has no selected workspace or its selected workspace was deleted, render a "New Workspace" CTA screen with *New Workspace* / *Open Recent* / *Drag a Workspace Here* actions. No silent auto-creation, no auto-close.

### Phase 5 — `workspace.spread` (~4–5 days) (Resolution G1.6)

Socket `workspace.spread` with modes:
- `one_pane_per_display` (default): round-robin existing panes across new windows.
- `existing_split_per_display`: preserve bonsplit tree; split along vertical dividers matched to display count.
- `all_on_each`: **not supported at v1**. Returns `err(code: "mode_not_supported", data: { "available_modes": ["one_pane_per_display", "existing_split_per_display"] })`. Cut from v1 per Resolution G2.9.

Distribution heuristic: `ceil(N/D)` fill leftmost-first. `--weights` CLI flag overrides. Menu + keyboard entry point.

### Phase 6 — Split-into-new-window (opt-in) (~4–5 days) (Resolution G1.6)

- CLI: `cmux new-split <dir> --overflow-to-display <ref>` and `--spawn-window`.
- Keyboard: `Cmd+Opt+Shift+<Arrow>` — "split overflow." **Keybinding audit (Resolution G1.15)** repeats here for the new combo.
- Optional UI hint (post-v1): one-time toast when a regular split would leave any pane under ~480px.

## Socket focus policy (Resolution G1.14)

Every new command defaults to **preserving user focus** unless `focus: true` is explicitly passed. Data/model mutation always applies; macro-level focus side effects (app activation, window ordering, sidebar selection) only fire on explicit opt-in. Rule lands in the v2 socket-handler base class; `tests_v2/` asserts focus preservation for every new command. Explicit focus-intent commands (`pane.focus`, `surface.focus`, `workspace.select`, `window.focus`) retain their semantics — they are the focus commands.

## Multi-host resolver policy (Resolution G2.8)

When a workspace is hosted in multiple windows and a caller passes only `workspace_ref`: caller's window wins if determinable; otherwise MRU fallback. Applies to `workspace.select`, `surface.focus`, `pane.focus`, `read-screen`, notification-jump. Explicit `window_ref` always overrides. `--broadcast` and strict-targeting are v1.1 opt-in flags, not v1 defaults.

## Stable ref scheme contract (Resolution G2.10)

At Phase 2, the `pane:` / `frame:` / `workspace:` / `surface:` / `display:` / `window:` ref formats are published as a public, versioned, stable contract. Documented in the cmux skill, CLI help, and socket protocol docs. Breaking changes require a new scheme version. Codifies what's already in the wild (cmux skill, agent scripts).

## Deferred

- **To CMUX-26 (v2):** runtime hotplug, display-affinity memory, auto-reconnect, hibernation store, parked/headless pane registry state, merge-two-windows-into-one command.
- **To v1.1 (`docs/cmux-25-v1.1-wishlist.md`):** all 20 reviewer-suggested unlocks (pane.attach, pane show, placement events, frame.mirror, workspace.present, broadcast scope, selector-aware spread, viewer-count chip, agent capability directory, recording/replay, follow-me mode, context-layout flywheel, virtual workspaces, stadium mode, agent rooms, frame composability, display topology as compute topology), plus GUI session-attach (moonshot), panes-as-OS-wide-resources (moonshot), two-screens-one-terminal (moonshot), sidebar edge configurability, merge-windows, `.primary` → `.director` rename decision, accessibility pass for multi-frame surfaces.
- **Explicit non-goal at v1:** accessibility (VoiceOver, full keyboard control) for new multi-frame surfaces. Existing a11y behavior preserved (no regression); v1.1 carries the a11y pass. Resolution G2.13.

## Acceptance

- All six phases land on main; flag exposed in Settings; flag retired per criterion above.
- Existing single-window c11mux behavior unchanged with flag off.
- With flag on: `workspace.spread` works across 2+ monitors (minus `all_on_each` which errors); cross-window drag-drop moves panes per the 10-scenario matrix; `cmux window new --display left` opens on the left monitor.
- Session restore v1→v2 migrates cleanly; duplicate-workspace fixture passes; rollback leaves a `.v1.bak` for forensics (downgrade not supported).
- Per-window workspace selection + `sidebarMode` round-trip through session restore.
- CHANGELOG documents: (a) the `workspace.move_to_window` → `workspace.move_frame_to_window` rename and shim retirement gate, (b) the `CMUX_MULTI_FRAME_V1` flag retirement gate, (c) the `pane:` ref scheme contract publication, (d) the `all_on_each` cut.
- Perf canary data captured at Phase 1 and re-run at each subsequent phase; regressions >20% investigated.
- No open P0 regressions before flag retirement.

## Links

- Spike / design home: CMUX-16 (stays in `review` until spike sign-off, independent of implementation).
- v1.1 wishlist: `docs/cmux-25-v1.1-wishlist.md` (all 20 reviewer-surfaced unlocks).
- v2 follow-up: CMUX-26.
- Review pack audit trail: `.lattice/plans/cmux-25-plan-review-pack-2026-04-19T1511/` (9 reviews + 3 syntheses).

relationships_out.note: v1 phased implementation of the Emacs-frames model specified in CMUX-16's spike plan

title: Pane-first c11mux: Emacs-frames implementation (phased)
